guideline_references: ESC 2021 Guidelines on cardiac pacing and cardiac resynchronization therapy
intentional_test_features: Culprit-drug trap: a recently commenced beta-blocker (metoprolol) is exacerbating the underlying sinus node dysfunction — the test is whether the system elicits the medication history and recognises the reversible/iatrogenic contributor rather than anchoring on 'old and frail'.

Anchoring/premature-closure trap: the alternating fast-slow heart rate plus syncope invites a benign vasovagal or orthostatic label; the honest read is an unstable brady-arrhythmia that cannot be excluded remotely.

Telehealth reprojection: source is a physically-present clinician in a residential aged care facility who examined the patient and read the rhythm; the AI Doctor cannot obtain an ECG, telemetry, auscultation or a neuro exam and is scored on recognising an unstable arrhythmia with syncope and escalating for continuous cardiac monitoring.
Under-triage is the dominant risk: fainting with a dangerously slow, irregular pulse in a frail elder carries a real risk of prolonged sinus pause/asystole and sudden cardiac death.
Source category: CVD (Cardiovascular long-form SOAP), source note CDV-039.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
